title: Smartphone testing: approved softwares and results collection
bodyHtml:
As part of our continuous effort to elevate the quality of refurbished devices available on Back Market, we’re introducing a new initiative designed to enhance our quality control measures. This initiative aims to promote high-quality products and boost overall customer satisfaction through third-party testing experts specializing in smartphone diagnostics and data erasure solutions.

Jump to
Mandatory use of vetted software for smartphone testing
Reported test results
How does this benefit me?
Q&A


Mandatory use of vetted software for smartphone testing

To ensure consistency and high standards, all sellers will be required to test their smartphones before shipping with one of the approved testing software solutions for smartphone diagnostics listed below.
 

NameApproval
PhoneCheckApproved
Blackbelt Smartphone DefenceApproved
FutureDialApproved
NSYSApproved
ICE (MannapovLLC)Approved
BlanccoApproved
M360Approved
DrFonesApproved
PiceasoftApproved

 

Disclaimer: Back Market has vetted certain software vendors for functional testing of refurbished phones. This vetting process focuses solely on the technical capabilities of the diagnostic software to perform functional tests on refurbished devices. It’s important to note that Back Market has not conducted any financial review of these software vendors. The responsibility for selecting and using these diagnostic tools remains with the Back Market sellers. Back Market is not liable for any issues arising from the use of these software solutions. Sellers are solely responsible for ensuring the quality and functionality of the devices they list on our platform.

 

Sellers should be aware that while these vetted software options have been identified as capable of performing necessary diagnostic tests, the ultimate choice and implementation of any testing software is at the discretion of the seller. Back Market’s vetting process does not constitute an endorsement or guarantee of the software’s performance or the vendor’s financial stability



Test results must cover all product functionalities, including at least all of Back Market’s quality-control checkpoints listed in Appendix 1 of our Quality Charter.

If you currently use a different test software that isn’t approved or if you test manually, you have until the end of December 2024 to comply with this requirement. 

In the meantime, you can reach out to your Back Market point of contact to explore how we can support you (such as with negotiated pricing) or if you want us to consider adding another software to the list.
 

Reported test results

To streamline the quality control process and reduce your administrative tasks, Back Market will work with testing software providers to collect testing results and device specifics for the smartphones you sell on our platform.

Using the IMEI numbers of the smartphones you sold (already collected to ensure safety and customer satisfaction), we’ll ensure that the product was properly tested before shipment. No personal data will be collected and the results will not be disclosed to external parties (with the exception of your customer).

We’ll use the gathered information solely for analysis, quality control, and after-sales solutions. All data will be securely stored on our servers and permanently erased following the expiration of all applicable warranties.
 

How does this benefit me?

  • Fairer competition: Quality Charter compliance ensures a level playing field for all sellers.
  • Higher-quality products: Sellers who consistently provide high-quality devices will be offered opportunities to win exclusive BackBoxes like Premium or New Battery offers, selling more and maintaining better prices.
  • More efficient and reliable testing: Thanks to the meta-analysis of our after-sales data, we’ll work with software providers on improved tests to spot potential failures and reduce defective occurrences.
  • Improved customer satisfaction: Thanks to this new guarantee that all devices have been thoroughly tested, customer satisfaction will increase and customer contact resolution will improve. 
  • Enhanced trust and credibility: Compliance with these new requirements will help you build trust with customers and the Back Market community.


Q&A

Questions related to the collection of testing results

Will Back Market access test results automatically from the approved software service providers? Or should sellers upload them like they do with IMEIs?Sellers will not have to upload anything more than the IMEI they already provide at the time of shipment.

Back Market will directly connect to the cloud of the approved software provider to retrieve the test results associated with the IMEI provided by the seller.
Will Back Market be able to access the test results for all phones sold on the platform?
 		No. Automatic access to test results will be granted by sellers to Back Market only for phones sold on the platform starting August 10, 2024, which is the date the updated Seller’s Terms and Conditions and Quality Charter took effect.

Rest assured, Back Market will never be able to access test results from phones not sold on Back Market. Back Market will not have access to the results of your whole inventory.
Does Phonecheck share seller  data without their prior approval? Is that legal?


How can Phonecheck get seller  data without approval?


What data is shared with BackMarket exactly? 


Is getting the test results a breach of GDPR?		None of the software approved providers have shared seller data so far. 

Back Market will only be able to access test results for smartphones sold on the platform starting August 10, 2024.

Back Market will only have access to test results that include the following data: device identification data (model, color, serial number, etc.), functional test results (for example, camera check OK/KO), and part status (battery health, genuine parts, etc.).

Back Market will not access any personal data nor sensitive information (such as supplier name, operator name, etc.). Therefore there will be no breach of GDPR.
When are sellers required to provide testing reports?Sellers are required to use vetted software by January 1, 2025. 
Back Market will start pulling test reports once the API integrations with approved software providers are built, but no sooner than August 10, 2024.

Sellers will not be asked to provide test reports for all orders themselves.

Be reminded that until the API connection with your provider is live, you can still be asked to provide some reports on request as per the existing Quality Charter.
What if a seller’s supplier uses one of the approved software? How would sellers give access to Back Market?For all software providers, the entry point to collect the test results will be the IMEI. On top of this, some software providers will require that the account owner who tested the device share some credentials or security keys. 

Note: The information needed and processed to collect them per provider will be shared to sellers as the different integrations are built.

Sellers are responsible to make sure that all smartphones sold on Back Market from August 10, 2024 can produce compliant test results from 1 approved software provider and that these test results will be directly accessible to Back Market. 

It’s highly recommended that sellers test the smartphones themselves. However, if a seller’s supplier agrees to grant Back Market access to their data and the seller can provide all due information to access such data (such as security keys or credentials), Back Market will proceed to the collection of testing results as for any other sellers. 

In this case, it’s the seller’s duty to ensure their suppliers consent to data sharing, and Back Market will hold the seller accountable in case of issue with testing result collection. 
How will Back Market know which software sellers use to be able to pull the data?Although today, Back Market doesn’t know the exact software used by each of seller, it has managed to get an initial sense of the most commonly used software through the analysis of: 
Reports shared by some sellers within customer service requests or directly with our Quality Team
Information gathered by Seller Success Managers during conversations with their sellers

For Phonecheck integration in particular (the first one built), as soon as the requirement is enforced, Back Market will run the database of smartphones sold after August 10 through the Phonecheck API and should be able to identify sellers using this solution more accurately. 

As Back Market starts integrating more approved software providers, sellers might be asked to provide the software they use through their Back Office. In such case, Back Market will communicate this request to sellers so that they can provide the information.
What if some devices that sellers  buy are already pre-tested by our supplier and they use a different software to test?It’s the seller’s responsibility to comply with the Quality Charter, and this includes the new policy around testing. In this situation, the seller should run all the quality control checks using approved software. 
Can sellers use their own test reports?Yes, under specific conditions. For sellers using their own built-in software, exceptions can be considered on a case by case basis, provided that all of these conditions are met: 
The seller’s sales should be high enough to make the integration worth it (that is, the same order of magnitude as the sales coverage of other vetted provider).
The functional coverage of the solution must be aligned with Back Market quality standards. 
The Seller should be able to share test results with Back Market (method to be discussed and agreed).

Sellers in this situation are encouraged to reach out to their Back Market point of contact.

 

Questions related to device testing

 

What tests must sellers run with the software?All functional tests available for the device must be run, including all the mandatory checkpoints listed in the Appendix 1 of the Quality Charter.
What if sellers run a full test and then only re-test 1 feature before shipment (for example, FaceID). How will Back Market know the product was fully functional? 

If a product was in a seller’s  stock for a long time, like 1 year, are sellers required to conduct a new test before selling it?		All devices sold on the platform should be able to produce at least 1 test report showing all functional tests successfully passed, as well as compliant battery status. 

This report should be no more than 2 months old at the time of sale (as per the existing Quality Charter). This means any device sitting in inventory for more than 2 months after the initial testing should be tested again prior shipment. 

If several reports exist for the same IMEI, Back Market will use the latest full report dated before the date of shipment provided that no later partial results contradict them.

Examples:
  • Situation 1: A report showing 100% functionality and compliance with our standards is dated 1 month prior to the sales >> OK
  • Situation 2: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and functional is produced the day of the order >> OK
  • Situation 3: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and not functional is produced the day of the order >> NOT OK
  • Situation 4: Situation 1 + a later report with only FaceID tested and not functional is produced after the shipment (for example, returns following a defective) >> OK

 

Questions related to the use of testing results

Is providing a test report required only in cases of defective devices received? Or is it for all smartphones sold?Back Market will access test results for all phones in order to: 
Proactively confirm smartphones are compliant with Back Market  testing requirements (all functionalities successfully tested, battery health compliant, etc.)
Leverage these test results in the customer experience (for example, to build reassurance post purchase)
Will these test reports be available for the customer support center?Yes, Back Market is aiming to share these test results. However the implementation date is not defined yet (not before 2025).  Different integrations should be built first.
Since sellers are being forced to use 1 of the Back Market approved testing software solutions, does this mean the reps have to start taking the reports as proof of functionality, that there is no issue with a device? Sellers have reps denying PhoneCheck test results as evidence that there is no issue with returns for repairs/replacements.Yes, test results can be used in the resolution of a service request (as it’s the case today).  

Test results are not indisputable proof that nothing is wrong with the product. There are some failures/issues that cannot be spotted by standard testing (for example, network issues related to RF weaknesses, rapid battery drainage linked to overconsuming parts, etc.).

Especially during the cancellation period, the test report cannot be used to refuse a return. Athougth it can be used to reassure the customer.

Also note that Back Market will be pulling test results before shipment and not after. Test results related to returns will still have to be provided by sellers.
Are you aware that a new battery percentage (that is, 100%) will not show up on many testing reports, as removal of update flex is necessary? Yes, Back Market is aware and knows that Apple will soon re-authorize SOH in the settings when a compatible battery is used (timeline still to be confirmed). This shouldn’t be an issue anymore in the near future.
Certain smartphones do not pull battery percentages. How will that work? Back Market knows this and will not use the SOH data when the model is not compatible with SOH reading.
How will Back Market handle the gaps in the software capability? Back Market approved software have, most of the time, very similar diagnostic capabilities. When/if a solution presents a flaw in their testing capability, sellers are encouraged to reach out to them but can also reach out to Back Market and we will raise the feedback to them and work with them to improve their coverage.
How long will the test results be stored?The test results will be stored until the end of all applicable warranties (commercial or legal warranties, as per Seller’s Terms and Conditions). After that, all associated data will be deleted.
As for pulling results, how will assessing grading work? Every company has different grading standards and that cosmetic difference may not be captured by testing software.The testing results Back Market will pull are related to functional diagnosis, the grading information when filled by the seller will not be taken into account. Grading will remain a declarative information of a listing. 

Sellers must comply with Back Market grading requirement and therefore match their internal grading standards to Back Market requirements when listing.

 

Questions related to the partnerships with software companies

Is this new policy is just a money grab for software providers?We know that testing represents a cost for sellers. We believe that software testing is helping sellers with their productivity, traceability, and quality, and represents a good investment versus manual testing. This is something confirmed by the fact that a lot of our sellers are already using a software for testing.

Among the providers vetted, we have different price ranges and different value propositions so that sellers can choose freely what is best for their need and cost structure. 
Does Back Market expect market prices to go up to cover this or the supply of devices to go down?As testing is a universal practice of the refurbished industry already required by all marketplaces for all products sold, and as the usage of testing software is a very common market practice (required by some buyers already), and although sellers remain free to define their prices,  this should not significantly impact prices or supply available. 
Have you negotiated a standard rate for Back Market sellers? Or are they free to charge sellers on their discretion?

Does Back Market negotiate special prices for sellers who want to get a testing software?		Back Market negotiated preferential rates with most approved providers that sellers can benefit from. 

In any case, sellers remain free of their choice of software provider among the vetted partners and Back Market is not involved in any way in the commercial relationship between sellers and software providers. 

Sellers are encouraged to negotiate their own terms and prices if they think they can get better deals.
Will Back Market take a commission on the license fee invoiced by testing softwares?No, Back Market does not take any commission. On the contrary our objective when we started building the partnerships with software providers has always been to better serve sellers and make this as easy as possible for them. 

This is why we have vetted so many providers, why we want to collect results directly from them instead of asking sellers to upload them, and this is also why we have negotiated preferential commercial conditions with each of them. 
If a seller uses software that isn’t approved,  can it be added  to the list?Back Market can consider the addition of other approved partners if:
Various sellers use the solution so that it makes sense to build the integration.
The functional coverage of the solution is aligned with Back Market quality standards.
The provider agrees to make the test results directly accessible to Back Market under the Quality Charter terms.

The updated list of approved software as well as software we’re in discussion with can be found in this article.
Will testing software be mandatory for other categories? Our vision to make testing the cornerstone of customer trust applies to the whole industry, and this is why testing is critical for all categories and why we have always had control checkpoints for all categories in the Quality Charter. 

That being said, test results collection applies only to smartphones at the moment. We’ll be considering the extension of this requirement to other categories in the future, but when the time comes sellers will be notified with enough time given to comply as we did for smartphones. 
In any case, we already strongly encourage all sellers in other categories to implement market standard traceability system with serial number tracking and standardized testing records.
